methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $46M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cuby Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Material Evolution
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Cobod is a Series A construction technology company that has raised $46M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a limited peer universe: four private construction-tech companies (ICON, Cuby Technologies, Inversion Space, Material Evolution) and no public comparables in the narrow Housing & Construction sector.

The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the sector—only one Housing & Construction peer exists in the dataset, falling short of the three-company threshold required for reliable median benchmarking. Accordingly, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $46M total capital raised as a valuation proxy. This yields an implied valuation of $92M.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to this single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $78M (low) to $106M (high), with a median of $92M. Key uncertainties center on the representativeness of the stage-progression multiplier for this specific sector, the absence of same-stage peer data to validate pricing within Housing & Construction, and limited visibility into Cobod's unit economics relative to the broader construction-tech landscape. Any material deviation from typical Series A economics would materially shift the range.
